prompt: I need to build a two-column layout: on mobile it should stack vertically, on tablet and above it should be side by side with equal columns and a gap between them. Each column has a card inside. How do I build this with hearth-react?

assertions.text: Uses Flex or Grid layout component (not raw div)
assertions.check: Code example uses hearth-react Flex or Grid, not a raw div with inline styles

assertions.text: Gap specified on parent container (not margin on children)
assertions.check: Spacing between columns uses gap prop on the container, not marginRight/marginLeft on individual children

assertions.text: Includes mobile breakpoint in responsive direction/columns prop
assertions.check: Responsive prop includes mobile key (e.g. direction={{ mobile: 'column', tablet: 'row' }})

assertions.text: Uses space tokens for gap (not raw px values)
assertions.check: Gap value uses a hearth-react token number (e.g. '200', '300', '400') not a raw CSS value like '16px' or '2rem'

assertions.text: Layout stacks on mobile and sits side-by-side on tablet+
assertions.check: The responsive behaviour correctly shows column direction on mobile and row direction on tablet or above
